content.text: Be neat, do not write too small. Show your work and support your statements.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(f,g:X\to Y\) be continuous maps. Define what it means for \(f\) to be homotopic to \(g\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Suppose that the continuous maps \(f,g:X\to Y\) are homotopic and that the continuous maps \(h,k:Y\to Z\) are homotopic. Show that \(h\circ f\) is homotopic to \(k\circ g\).

problem.text: If \(j:A\hookrightarrow X\) is the inclusion of a retract, then show that the induced map on fundamental groups is injective.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Determine the fundamental group of \(S^1\vee S^1\).

problem.text: Prove that there are infinitely many distinct homotopy types of spaces with one 0-cell, one 1-cell, and one 2-cell.
Let \(\mathbb{RP}^2\) denote the real projective plane.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give the universal cover of \(\mathbb{RP}^2\) including the covering map.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: What is the group, \(G\), of covering transformations of this cover?

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: How does it act on the universal cover?

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Does \(\mathbb{RP}^2\) have a cover, \(p:E\to\mathbb{RP}^2\), whose fundamental group, \(\pi_1(E)\), is \(\mathbb{Z}/(3)\)? Explain why or why not.

subparts.label: (e)
subparts.text: Does \(\mathbb{RP}^2\) have a cover, \(p':E'\to\mathbb{RP}^2\), whose covering transformation group, \(G(E')\), is \(\mathbb{Z}/(5)\)? Explain why or why not.

problem.text: Give the definition of a chain complex of abelian groups and its homology groups.
Let \((C,d)\), and \((C',d')\) be chain complexes of abelian groups.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for \(f:(C,d)\to(C',d')\) to be a morphism of chain complexes, also called a chain map.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: For \(n\in\mathbb{Z}\) define the induced map \(H_n(f):H_n(C,d)\to H_n(C',d')\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(X\) be a topological space. Define the singular chain complex \(\mathbf{S}_\bullet(X)\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(X\) be the one-point space \(*\). Compute \(H_n(X)\) for all \(n\).

problem.text: Use the cellular chain complex to compute the homology of the Klein bottle.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State the Mayer–Vietoris sequence for reduced singular homology. You do not need to define the connecting homomorphism.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(n\geq 0\). Compute the reduced singular homology groups \(\widetilde{H}_k(S^n)\) for all \(k\).
